paper_focus.title: Microstructures, mechanical properties and deformation of near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr steels
paper_focus.research_objective: To determine the influence of Cr addition on the microstructures, mechanical properties, and deformation behavior of near-rapidly solidified austenite-based low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C steels.
material_systems: Austenite-based duplex steel containing δ-ferrite and κ-carbide precipitates
main_process_variables: Chemical composition: Cr content of 0, 3, 6 wt.%
Near-rapid solidification: centrifugal casting in argon atmosphere, copper mold rotating at 600 rpm, cooling rate ~1–1×10^4 K/s
Stress relief annealing: 400 °C for 1 h before cold rolling
Cold rolling: reduction of 20% in thickness (multipass) to obtain CR20%
Post-rolling annealing: CR20% strips annealed at 600 °C for 3 h (CR20%+annealed)
